response:
SECTION 1
The squirrel claims no nuts exist in the other hollow.

SECTION 2
It also states at least one nut is present somewhere.


SECTION 3
If this creature were gray, both statements would be false.

SECTION 4
A false first claim means nuts are actually in the other hollow.

SECTION 5
A false second claim means zero nuts exist in either hollow.

SECTION 6
These two outcomes contradict each other, so the squirrel must be red.
